checked_sources: OpenStax Elementary Algebra 2e Ch 3.5 (Relations & Functions) reference listed in course JSON
Khan Academy Evaluating Functions practice reference listed in course JSON
findings: Module title, order, and objective in script.json match the published Algebra I module 7 in server/prisma/courses/math/algebra-i.json: 'Introduction to Functions' with objectives covering function definition, domain and range, evaluation, function notation, and the function/non-function distinction.

Assignment described in the lesson (5 relations to classify as function or not with one-sentence reasons; 10 evaluations across 3 functions showing substitution; domain and range listed for each tested function) matches the assignment string in the source packet.

Resources referred to by name (OpenStax Elementary Algebra 2e Ch 3.5 and Khan Academy evaluating-functions practice) match the resources listed in the course JSON and the source packet; no URLs are read aloud in narration.
No AP, College Board, Common App, CEEB, NCAA, accreditation, or admissions claims appear in lesson metadata, slide text, or narration.

No sourced or generated bitmap images are used. All precision visuals — input-output machine diagrams, mapping diagrams, the function-vs-non-function comparison, the worked-evaluation traces, and the domain/range table — are drawn deterministically in build_slides.py, so no assets_manifest.json is required.

Narration consistently uses 'f of x' instead of reading the parentheses as multiplication, and pronounces 'domain' and 'range' as sets, which keeps the audio track unambiguous when the slide is not visible.
